dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 2804 RH: Protecting Small Business Competitions Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Velázquez introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Small Business
Additional sponsors: Mr. Cisneros, Ms. Davids of Kansas, Mr. Case, and Mr. Williams of Texas
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on April 9, 2025

form.official-title: To amend the Small Business Act to codify the Rule of Two, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Small Business Competitions Act of 2026.

section.header: Codification of the rule of two
section.text: Section 15(j) of the Small Business Act (15 U.S.C. 644(j)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

subparagraph.text: Each covered contract for goods or services that has an anticipated value greater than the simplified acquisition threshold shall be reserved for small business concerns when the contracting officer reasonably expects—

clause.text: to obtain offers from two or more responsible small business concerns; and
to award such contract at a fair market price.

subparagraph.text: In this paragraph, the term covered contract does not include a delivery order or a task order.

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
